data.title: Preplanning escalation policy has no observed impact during sprint execution
data.description:
## Problem
The escalation policy selected during preplanning (Phase 1 Step 1b) is written into story descriptions but has no observed behavioral impact during sprint skill execution. The policy text is passive documentation — no enforcement mechanism consumes it.

Specific observed failures:
1. When **Autonomous** is selected, explicit user approval is still required to edit protected/safeguard files during sprint execution. The sprint orchestrator and sub-agents do not read the escalation policy from the story ticket to adjust their approval gates.
2. When **Escalate unless confident** is selected, sub-agents do not raise questions to the user during implementation when intent is not clearly described in the epic/story. The policy text exists in the story but is not consumed by the implementation-plan or sprint skills to modify agent behavior.

## Root Cause
The escalation policy is stored as text in the story description but is never parsed or acted upon by downstream skills (/dso:implementation-plan, /dso:sprint). There is no mechanism that:
- Reads the escalation policy from the story ticket
- Adjusts approval gates (e.g., safeguard file edits) based on the selected policy
- Modifies sub-agent dispatch prompts to include policy-appropriate escalation behavior

## Expected Behavior
- **Autonomous**: Sub-agents proceed with best judgment. Protected file edit approval gates should be relaxed — the user has pre-authorized autonomous decision-making. Questions about intent should not block progress.
- **Escalate when blocked**: Sub-agents proceed unless a significant assumption could send implementation in the wrong direction. Protected file approvals still required but with lower friction.
- **Escalate unless confident**: Sub-agents actively raise questions to the user when high confidence is absent. Implementation pauses for clarification rather than proceeding with inference.

## Expected Fix
1. Sprint orchestrator and sub-agent dispatch prompts must read the escalation policy from the story ticket and include it as behavioral guidance
2. Implementation-plan task generation should embed the policy into task-level instructions
3. Protected file approval gates should consult the escalation policy — Autonomous mode should pre-authorize edits that the current workflow blocks on
4. Sub-agents dispatched with Escalate unless confident should include explicit instructions to pause and ask when intent is ambiguous
